objects.name: Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment
objects.description: Attackers send spear-phishing emails spoofed as official Income Tax Department notices with malicious PDF attachments to gain initial access.

objects.name: Phishing: Spearphishing Link
objects.description: PDF attachment directs user to a fake ITD portal URL, triggering auto-download of malware ZIP.

objects.name: User Execution
objects.description: Victim manually runs extracted executable setup_Ir5swQ3EpeuBpePEpew=.exe.

objects.name: Signed Binary Proxy Execution
objects.description: Digitally signed NSIS installers are used to appear legitimate and evade detection.

objects.name: Subvert Trust Controls: Code Signing
objects.description: Chinese code-signed payloads are used to bypass security checks.

objects.name: Artifact Deletion
objects.description: First-stage loader deletes dropped files and temp folders after execution to evade detection.

objects.name: Obfuscated/Encrypted Payloads
objects.description: Multi-stage NSIS wrapping with hidden components and protected directories for defense evasion.

objects.name: Create/Modify System Process: Windows Service
objects.description: NSecRTS.exe registered as a persistent background service using Windows Real-time Protection Service name.

objects.name: Boot/Logon Autostart Execution
objects.description: Service automatically runs at boot without user interaction for persistence.

objects.name: System Information Discovery
objects.description: Harvests OS version, machine details, hardware, and services from victim machine.

objects.name: Software Discovery
objects.description: Collects installed application details from victim machine.

objects.name: Web Protocols
objects.description: Uses HTTP/HTTPS with UID registration to maintain network communication with C2.

objects.name: Non-Application Layer Protocol
objects.description: C2 uses multiple non-standard ports (48991/48992/3898 etc.) for communication.

objects.name: Commonly Used Port
objects.description: Alternative fallback communication via port 80 on two additional C2 IPs.

objects.name: Exfiltration Over Web Services
objects.description: Harvested telemetry posted back via HTTP POST to primary C2.

objects.name: Scheduled Transfer
objects.description: Data uploaded regularly as suspicious process continues to run in the background.

objects.name: Remote Access Tools
objects.description: NSecRTS.exe enables remote command reception, tasking, and persistence.

objects.name: NSecRTS.exe RAT Indicators
objects.description: Indicators associated with NSecRTS.exe RAT campaign targeting Indian businesses.
objects.pattern: [file:hashes.'MD5' = '4001854be1ae8e12b6dda124679a4077' OR file:hashes.'MD5' = 'F00F824FCAFBA9B26675AE8242F0B6A0' OR url:value = 'hxxps://www.akjys.top/' OR ipv4-addr:value = '154.91.84.3' OR ipv4-addr:value = '45.113.192.102' OR ipv4-addr:value = '103.235.46.102']

objects.name: Indian Income Tax-Themed Phishing and RAT Delivery
objects.description: A multi-stage phishing and malware campaign targeting Indian businesses during the Income Tax Return (ITR) filing season. Attackers impersonate the Income Tax Department, using spear-phishing emails, malicious PDFs, and fake portals to deliver NSecRTS.exe RAT.
objects.objective: Compromise Indian business endpoints for remote access, data exfiltration, and persistent control.
